title: Gilibertia pendula
taxonomy: Gilibertia pendula is classified within the plant family Asphodelaceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaves and flowering structure. Gilibertia pendula is native to New Zealand and is a part of the Asphodelaceae family that includes plants like asphodels and yams. The Asphodelaceae family is a group of monocots, meaning they have one cotyledon in their seedling stage. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships between different plant species within the Asphodelaceae family.
